channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Blu-ray is dying so I believe it's now time for 'obsolete' tape to shine again in the strangest of ironies
item.description: Sony announced that it is shutting down Blu-ray media production forever, so is tape the alternative?
item.dc:creator: desire.athow@futurenet.com (Desire Athow), TechRadar

item.title: How the broligarchy is imitating Trump in more ways than one
item.description: Sooner or later, the politicians who most admire Donald Trump begin to emulate him. They adopt his populist rhetoric, specific diction, or aggressive tone. If they happen to be men, they might stuff their closets with navy suits and red ties. Some of them even start to stand like him.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Bun 1.2 squashes Node.js compatibility bugs
item.description: Bun 1.2, an update to the all-in-one runtime and toolkit for building, running, and testing full-stack JavaScript and TypeScript applications, has arrived, boasting dramatic improvements in Node.js compatibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: We need to put human creativity at the center of adtech
item.description: I’ve been searching for the words to describe my feelings towards the current state of adtech. Terms like “stale,” “stagnant,” and “boring” are among the pejoratives that come to mind.
item.dc:creator: Chris Gadek, Fast Company

item.title: How dangerous are 3D printers? Maybe enough for a background check
item.description: As 3D-printed gun violence abounds, some lawmakers are looking to cut the problem at the root. The New York state senate is currently evaluating a bill that would dramatically change the 3D printing market.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Meta nearly doubling spending with eye toward AI
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced Friday the social media company will nearly double its spending this year as it focuses on artificial intelligence (AI) development. Meta is planning to invest $60 billion to $65 billion in 2025, Zuckerberg said Friday in a post on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: New bill would allow California communities to decide where battery plants are located
item.description: AB 303 will remove battery energy storage facilities from the California Energy Commission’s opt-in certification program, and return the power to local communities on whether to accept new battery plants.
item.dc:creator: Kyarra Harris, The Mercury News

item.title: World2Meet redefine la asistencia al viajero con un innovador avatar hiperrealista
item.description: En el marco de la celebración de la edición 2025 de Fitur, la capital vuelve a convertirse en una suerte de escaparate de alcance mundial para las empresas dedicadas en cuerpo y alma al sector hospitality.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Oval Office ‘Stargate Project’ reveal was just more tech industry genuflecting
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he will use energy emergency declaration to approve more power for AI
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that he will use his energy emergency declaration to approve power stations for artificial intelligence (AI) centers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: 3 hidden benefits of Dedicated Internet Access for enterprises
item.description: Reliable Internet connectivity is crucial for businesses to function efficiently and remain competitive in today’s digital world. While standard broadband connections may work for basic tasks, they often struggle to meet the demands of modern businesses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing crypto working group
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a working group on digital assets led by David Sacks, the White House czar for artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptocurrency.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Race is on to finish San Jose hotel in time for major Nvidia conference
item.description: Developers hope to gallop to the finish line to complete a San Jose hotel in time for a big Nvidia conference downtown.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Linkedin sued for training AI on users’ private messages
item.description: A lawsuit in California accuses Linkedin of using private messages on its platform to train AI models, according to the BBC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN
item.description: Lt. Gen. Paul Stanton, commander of JFHQ-DoDIN, said commanders can better manage risks and focus on defending the applications and data that matters the most. The post Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek unveils open-source model to rival OpenAI o1
item.description: Chinese AI developer DeepSeek has unveiled an open-source version of its reasoning model, DeepSeek-R1, featuring 671 billion parameters and claiming performance superior to OpenAI’s o1 on key benchmarks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Taiwan plans to cull up to 120,000 green iguanas due to their negative impact on agriculture
item.description: Taiwan plans to cull up to 120,000 green iguanas, with supporters urging humane methods to bring down the animal's population, which is wreaking havoc on the island's agricultural sector.
item.dc:creator: Tai-Jing Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From devops to CTO: 5 things to start doing now
item.description: I was promoted to CTO in my late twenties, and while it is common to see young CTOs leading startups these days, it was unusual in the ’90s. I was far less experienced back then, and still developing my business acumen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok goes dark as US ban takes effect
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC
